name: Profanity-Filtering-All-languages
description: Supports: en en_au en_uk en_us af sq am ar hy as az ba eu be bn bs br bg my ca ceb zh hr cs da nl et fo fi fr gl ka de de_ch el gu ht ha haw he hi hu is ig id it ja jw kn kk km ko ku lo la lv ln lt lb mk mg ms ml mt mi mr mn ne no nn oc pa ps fa pl pt ro ru sa sr sn sd si sk sl so es su sw sv tl tg ta tt te th bo tr tk uk ur uz vi cy yi yo zu
readme_summary: This TypeScript library provides a highly performant profanity filtering solution supporting 107 languages. It offers features like lazy loading, zero runtime dependencies, and a rich API for checking, detecting, and censoring profanity, with options for customization.
key_features: Rich API (check, detect, censor, addWords, removeWords)
primary_use_case: To filter out or censor profane language in text across a wide range of languages for applications like chat platforms, content moderation, or user-generated content.
cover_image_prompt: A wise, ancient librarian meticulously sorting and cleaning a vast library filled with books in various languages. The librarian uses a magical sieve to filter out 'dirty' or 'corrupted' pages, which turn into harmless dust, while allowing clean pages to pass through. Subtle glowing digital particles representing data flow around the librarian and the books. The repository name 'profanity-filter-106' is subtly etched onto a nearby shelf. The scene is set in a warm, inviting library with soft, focused lighting, rendered in a detailed digital painting style with clear outlines.

name: clamshell
description: 🐚 A daemon that implements clamshell mode on Linux for any init system
readme_summary: Clamshell is a Linux daemon that enables clamshell mode, allowing users to close their laptop lid and continue working with external displays and peripherals without requiring AC power or a specific init system. It offers flexible suspend mode options and specific handling for NVIDIA GPUs.
primary_use_case: Enabling laptop users to close their lid and use external monitors seamlessly on Linux, regardless of power connection or init system.
cover_image_prompt: A laptop with its lid closed, but a vibrant, glowing portal opens from the screen, projecting a fully functional desktop environment onto a large external monitor. A user is comfortably typing on an external keyboard and using a mouse, seemingly unaware the laptop lid is shut. Subtle digital particles representing data flow from the closed laptop to the external display. The 'Clamshell' logo is subtly integrated into the portal's design. The scene is set in a cozy, modern workspace with soft, ambient lighting. The overall style is a clean, 3D isometric illustration with a focus on clear visual communication of the 'lid closed, external display active' concept.

name: sshb
description: SSH Buddy - Tamagotchi Terminal Pet Daemon
readme_summary: SSHB (SSH Buddy) is a terminal-based Tamagotchi-style virtual pet that lives in your command line. It requires regular care, with its stats decaying over time, and can be integrated into your bash prompt to display its status.
key_features: Tamagotchi-style virtual pet in the terminal
ASCII art pet with animated expressions
Four core stats: Hunger, Energy, Happiness, Health
Command quips based on exit codes
primary_use_case: To provide a fun, interactive Tamagotchi-like experience within the user's terminal environment, enhancing engagement with command-line usage.
cover_image_prompt: A charming, stylized ASCII art cat character sitting on a stack of glowing computer terminals. The cat is looking expectantly at the viewer, with small status bars (Hunger, Energy, Happiness) floating above its head, some showing low levels. In the background, a subtle digital rain effect cascades down, representing the passage of time and stat decay. A small, integrated bash prompt element is visible on one of the terminals, displaying the cat's current mood icon. The overall mood is playful and slightly urgent, encouraging interaction. The art style should be a clean, modern 3D isometric illustration with vibrant, glowing neon accents on a dark background.

name: kugelaudio-open
description: Open-source text-to-speech for European languages with voice cloning
readme_summary: KugelAudio is an open-source text-to-speech (TTS) system focused on European languages, leveraging an AR + Diffusion architecture. It aims to bridge the gap in high-quality TTS for underserved European languages, offering performance that rivals and surpasses leading commercial solutions.
key_features: Outperforms leading commercial TTS systems (e.g., ElevenLabs) in human preference benchmarks
Trained on 200,000 hours of speech data from the YODAS2 dataset
Supports diverse speaking styles including neutral, shouting, singing, and drunken voice
primary_use_case: Providing high-quality, open-source text-to-speech synthesis for a wide range of European languages, enabling developers and researchers to create natural-sounding speech for various applications.
cover_image_prompt: A master linguist, depicted as a skilled artisan, meticulously crafting unique, vibrant sound waves from simple text scrolls. The artisan uses glowing tools that represent advanced AI, transforming written European languages (shown as ancient scrolls with diverse scripts) into rich, expressive audio streams that flow like colorful ribbons. Subtle UI elements displaying waveform visualizations and the 'KugelAudio' logo are integrated into the artisan's workbench. The scene is set in a bright, modern workshop filled with the tools of both language and technology, bathed in a warm, inspiring light. The style should be a 3D isometric illustration with a clean, inviting aesthetic, emphasizing the transformation of text into lifelike speech.

name: dangerzone
description: Take potentially dangerous PDFs, office documents, or images and convert them to safe PDFs
readme_summary: Dangerzone is a security tool that converts potentially dangerous documents (PDFs, Office files, images) into safe, pixel-based PDFs. It achieves this by processing the documents within a sandboxed environment, converting them to raw pixel data, and then reconstructing them as new PDFs, thus neutralizing embedded threats.
key_features: Support for various document and image formats
primary_use_case: To safely convert untrusted documents into a secure PDF format, mitigating potential security risks from malicious content.
cover_image_prompt: A medieval alchemist in a secure laboratory, carefully placing a corrupted-looking scroll (representing an untrusted document) into a glowing, transparent containment field. Inside the field, the scroll is being deconstructed into pure, colorful light particles (representing pixel data). From the other side of the containment field, a pristine, sealed scroll (representing a safe PDF) emerges. Subtle digital circuit patterns are etched into the lab's surfaces, and a small, stylized Dangerzone logo is visible on the alchemist's apron. The scene is brightly lit with a focus on the transformation process, rendered in a 3D isometric illustration style with clean lines and a vibrant, contrasting color palette.
